anchor.snippet: a Euclidean group is the group of (Euclidean) isometries of a Euclidean space
The Euclidean group E( n ) comprises all translations
The direct Euclidean isometries form a subgroup, the special Euclidean group
The number of degrees of freedom for E( n ) is n ( n + 1)/2
The direct isometries (i.e., isometries preserving the handedness of chiral subsets) comprise a subgroup of E( n )

annotations.label: Indirect isometries form a coset; E+(n) has index 2

anchor.snippet: the indirect isometries are a coset of E + ( n )
a sequence f i of isometries of
Such a function is called a "continuous trajectory" in E( n )
the special Euclidean group SE( n ) = E + ( n ) is connected in this topology
the group E( n ) as a whole is not connected

annotations.label: Continuous trajectories model rigid body motion

anchor.snippet: The continuous trajectories in E(3) play an important role in classical mechanics
The Euclidean groups are not only topological groups , they are Lie groups

annotations.label: E(n) as subgroup of affine group / semidirect product

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean group E( n ) is a subgroup of the affine group for n dimensions

anchor.section: Subgroup structure, matrix and vector representation
anchor.snippet: Any element of E( n ) is a translation followed by an orthogonal transformation

anchor.section: Subgroup structure, matrix and vector representation
anchor.snippet: T( n ) is a normal subgroup of E( n )

annotations.label: E(n) is semidirect product O(n) ⋉ T(n)

anchor.section: Subgroup structure, matrix and vector representation
anchor.snippet: E( n ) is the semidirect product of O( n ) extended by T( n )

annotations.label: E+(n) is index-two subgroup of direct isometries

anchor.section: Subgroup structure, matrix and vector representation
anchor.snippet: E( n ) has a subgroup E + ( n ), also of index two, consisting of direct isometries

annotations.label: Finite subgroups have a fixed point

anchor.snippet: They always have a fixed point
for every point the set of images under the isometries is topologically discrete
there are points for which the set of images under the isometries is not closed

anchor.section: Overview of isometries in up to three dimensions
anchor.snippet: Chasles' theorem asserts that any element of E + (3) is a screw displacement
For some isometry pairs composition does not depend on order
The translations by a given distance in any direction form a conjugacy class
In 1D, all reflections are in the same class
In 2D, rotations by the same angle in either direction are in the same class
Inversions with respect to all points are in the same class
